last_item: Alerts — the rules-table 'Sustained' / 'Cooldown' headers are bare jargon; a user can't decode them without opening the editor (med/S).
last_result: SHIPPED a rename, not the proposed tooltip. The backlog item proposed Tooltip definitions on both headers; I implemented that first (InfoIcon trigger + TooltipProvider, definition mirrored into aria-label for a11y/testability) and ran the full panel: 3 SHIP (UX heuristics — recognition-over-recall; Sam — '"Sustained" next to "instant" genuinely puzzled me', shipped with a touch caveat that hover-only help is dead on mobile; Priya — muted/ignorable/out-of-the-way). RESTRAINT CRITIC BLOCKED (never overruled): the 'Cooldown' tooltip is decoration (cooldown + a duration value is self-explanatory) and the one real gap ('Sustained') doesn't need tooltip machinery + a hover-only affordance — just rename the header. The critic's alternative was clearly right and cheaper AND resolved Sam's touch caveat, so per the contract I addressed the blocker IN-ITERATION rather than rejecting: reverted the tooltip change entirely and renamed 'Sustained' -> 'Hold for', matching the rule editor's own 'Must hold for (minutes)' label (self-documenting, consistent, zero new surface). Left 'Cooldown' unchanged. Swapped the test to a columnheader assertion for 'Hold for'. Gates: typecheck OK, 138 tests OK, build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox — snap-confine blocks in-sandbox). Lesson recorded: when the header WORD is the jargon, fix the word — don't annotate a bad label with hover text; and when the restraint critic hands you a cheaper clearly-right alternative, take it in-iteration. Next top item: Pooler — reword the enable-confirmation copy so it's explicit that apps must be reconfigured to the pooler address (med/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
